macro_summary: US stock futures are mixed Monday after the long holiday weekend as paused US-Iran peace talks and SK Hynix's $28B Nasdaq listing jostle for attention. Friday's June payrolls miss (57k vs 129k expected) slammed the dollar and pushed gold above $4,200, but a jump in 6-month T-bill yields to 4% signals the bond market is still pricing rate-hike risk ahead of Wednesday's Fed minutes. WTI at $68.65 reflects a market convinced the Strait of Hormuz premium has evaporated, with OPEC+ supply more than offsetting the still-constricted waterway.
commodities_note: WTI near $68.65, down 28.5% over the past month as OPEC+ output surge and Asian demand destruction overwhelm the Hormuz supply cut. Gold holds above $4,200, buoyed by the dollar's 0.6% weekly decline and residual safe-haven demand.

SITM.stale_session_note: No new session since 2026-07-02 — bar date and price identical to the previous report (market closed: holiday/weekend). Technicals are unchanged, already-reported data.

writeup.headline: SiTime -12.5% below SMA50; MEMS timing thesis intact but 56x PE needs hyperscaler wins.
writeup.what_to_do: SiTime's MEMS timing displacement of quartz is a structural thesis, but 56x forward PE demands a named AI-timing design win — not yet public. R:R 1.7:1 to $769 offers little cushion. Revisit on a stabilization above the 200-day ($431) and a clarity on hyperscaler adoption.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 56x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain hard-blocked on extension with RSI above 65. The NIM compression thesis persists: US10Y at 4.49% still above the 4.20% pain threshold, and MAS kept S$NEER appreciation pace. OCBC continues to outperform on relative NIM resilience, but none are actionable until a 50-day retracement.
singapore.key_development: US10Y at 4.49% sustains the USD funding-cost headwind.

semiconductors.summary: The Semis cluster remains under heavy stress: SOXX fell 5.6% in the last session and is down 9.4% over the week. Mega-cap AI names (NVDA, ASML, MU) are either extended or below the SMA50, and ASML's approaching earnings add binary risk. SK Hynix's $28B Nasdaq listing is a bright spot — a re-rate catalyst once the stock consolidates.
semiconductors.key_development: SK Hynix $28B US listing could lift HBM valuations, but Semis need a macro catalyst to break the AI capex doubts.

big_tech.summary: BigTech AI thesis faces a split: Microsoft is hard-blocked after a 10.7% surge, Palantir's defense deals are overshadowed by a 152% cluster premium, while Amazon and Google hold near SMA50 as cheap defensive plays. The cluster's key risk is the hyperscaler capex ROI debate, amplified by Meta's new cloud push and the 400-newspaper lawsuit against Microsoft.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but ROI and revenue quality concerns linger.
big_tech.key_development: Meta's AI cloud entry threatens AWS/Azure market share; MSFT legal risk from copyright lawsuit.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA $2B photonics investment partner (alongside LITE) — AI datacenter optical interconnect supplier.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's photonics revenue commentary accelerates above $300M quarterly run-rate.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC designer competing with AVGO; data-center networking and optical DSPs benefit from AI capex.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist on a pullback of 8–10% from current levels if AVGO's custom ASIC backlog grows.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: High-speed datacenter switching for AI clusters — direct read on NVDA/AVGO infrastructure buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AI cluster capex re-accelerates after summer earnings season.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz disruption at 1/3 pre-war levels, but market priced surplus; US-Iran peace talks stalled.
US-China tech decoupling: Alibaba bans Anthropic coding tool, deepening AI rift; USMCA annual review adds trade friction.
Fed rate-hike debate: June payrolls miss (57k) tanked hawkish bets, but 6M T-bill at 4% signals bond market expects hikes.
Russia-Ukraine: Russia claims Donetsk logistics hub, Kyiv attacked; no imminent market impact.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil's de-escalation removes the energy premium from the portfolio; tech is pressured by US-China AI restrictions (Alibaba ban) and copyright lawsuits (MSFT). The payrolls-induced dollar weakness gave gold a lift above $4,200, but the bond market's rate-hike pushback is keeping US10Y near 4.49%, which sustains Singapore banks' NIM compression and caps BigTech multiple expansion.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US-Iran peace talks stalled; SK Hynix files for $28B US listing; 400-newspaper lawsuit against OpenAI/Microsoft; 6M T-bill yield hits 4%.

base.description: US-Iran detente holds, Strait throughput slowly recovers; WTI stabilises near $65. Semis trade on earnings fundamentals, with ASML/TSM earnings as the near-term drivers. Singapore banks remain range-bound on elevated US10Y.

optimistic.description: Hormuz fully reopens, oil crashes to $55, inflation collapses; Fed cuts rates in September. AI capex fears ease, NVDA and semis rally 15%, Singapore banks get NIM relief as US10Y drops below 4%.

pessimistic.description: Peace talks break down, Hormuz shuts again, WTI spikes to $100; Fed forced to hike again. Tech stocks sell off 10%, Singapore banks see NIM squeeze worsen, ASML China exposure re-priced.

wildcard.description: A major China semiconductor breakthrough triggers a sector-wide re-rating; ASML EUV monopoly questioned, semis plunge 20%.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new US entries until post-July 4 data settles; Asian names (SK Hynix, Singapore banks) are extended; wait for CPI/Fed minutes clarity.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 8 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; the rest are either in wait-for-earnings or below SMA50 with no actionable R:R. The semis selloff has washed out many entries, but the macro backdrop (rate-hike risk, AI capex doubts) keeps the cash-heavy posture intact.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
